📖 ABOUT

Science Quest: Academy of Physical Wonders

An immersive RPG covering Georgia Standards of Excellence for 8th Grade Physical Science (S8P1–S8P5).

Standards Covered:

  • S8P1 — Matter, Atoms & Periodic Table
  • S8P2 — Energy
  • S8P3 — Force, Mass & Motion
  • S8P4 — Waves
  • S8P5 — Gravity, Electricity & Magnetism

Phase 1 Build · Character Creator + Aetheryn Academy Starter Zone

Raw Particles
Purchase individual particles with gold. The forge cares not where they come from — only what you build.
Particle Exchange
Trade particles you have a surplus of for ones you need. The cosmos is in balance — your inventory should be too.
Pre-Measured Element Kits
Exactly the particles needed for one atom of each element. A small premium — but no counting needed.
🎢 THE KINETIC COASTER — Energy Identification
"Energy never disappears on this track — it just changes form. Find the right spot, and you'll see why."
🪙 0
⚡ 0/5 solved
⚡ THE LAW OF CONSERVATION OF ENERGY
On a frictionless coaster, total mechanical energy stays constant. As the cart climbs, kinetic energy (KE) converts to potential energy (PE). As it falls, PE converts back to KE. Their sum never changes.

Formula reminder:
  PE = mass × g × height  (here m = 100 kg, g = 9.8 m/s²)
  KE = Total Energy − PE

Read the 3 labeled spots to learn the total energy, then click the numbered candidate spots that match each prompt. Some prompts have ONE answer, others have multiple — read carefully!
⚡ Total Mechanical Energy on this Track: 0 J

⚗️ The Sorter's Workbench
"Every substance in the world is one of four things. Train your eye to know which."
Round 1/12
Score 0
Streak 0
How it works: Samples ride the conveyor belt across the top. Before each one drops off the end, click the chute that matches what it is.

Element — only one type of atom (e.g. pure copper).
Compound — two or more elements chemically bonded (e.g. water, salt).
Homogeneous Mixture — evenly blended, you cannot see separate parts (e.g. salt water, brass).
Heterogeneous Mixture — uneven, you CAN see separate parts (e.g. trail mix, salad).

You have 12 samples per session. Difficulty rises as you go. Score 9+ for a passing mark; perfect 12 earns the Sorter's Seal.
Sample incoming…
Watch the belt. Click a chute to sort.

🔬 The Change Identifier
"Watch the substance closely. Did it merely change shape — or did it become something new?"
Trial 1/10
Score 0
Streak 0
How it works: An animation plays inside the dome showing something happening to a substance. You must decide:

Physical change — the substance changes form but is still the same stuff. Reversible. No new substance made. Examples: melting, dissolving, chopping, bending, freezing.
Chemical change — a new substance is made. Usually not reversible. Look for evidence: color change, gas/bubbles, light or heat released, lasting smell.

If you call it Chemical, you must also pick the strongest evidence you saw. Some changes have more than one valid evidence — when that's true, any of them counts. Score 7+ for a passing mark; perfect 10 earns the Change-Reader's Mark.
Trial 1 of 10
Streak: 0
—
Step 1 — Identify the change
Was this a physical or a chemical change?
Step 2 — What was the strongest evidence?

🍳 Chef Calida's Heat Transfer Kitchen
"Watch closely as I cook. Tell me — by what means is the heat traveling into the food?"
Trial 1/10
Score 0
Streak 0
How it works: An animation plays inside the kitchen window showing food being cooked. You must identify which type of heat transfer is happening:

🔥 Conduction — heat moves through DIRECT CONTACT between objects (a pan touching a burner; a spoon in hot soup). Heat flows from hot to cold by particles bumping into each other.
💨 Convection — heat moves through a FLUID (liquid or gas) as warmer parts rise and cooler parts sink, creating a current. Think boiling water, baking in an oven, deep frying.
☀️ Radiation — heat travels as INVISIBLE WAVES (infrared light) through space, without needing contact or a fluid. Heat lamps, broilers, campfires warming your hands across the air.

Score 7+ for a passing mark. A perfect 10 earns the Chef's Mark.
Trial 1 of 10
Streak: 0

Identify how the heat is reaching the food.

❄️ The Particle Dance Floor
"Every substance is made of particles in motion. Heat is just how much they move."
Challenge 1/5
Score 0
How it works: Inside the chamber are tiny particles of a substance. Drag the temperature slider — watch how the particles respond.

Solid — particles vibrate in fixed positions (a lattice).
Liquid — particles slide past each other but stay close.
Gas — particles fly freely, filling the chamber.

You can pick three substances: water, liquid nitrogen, or iron — each has its own real melting and boiling points. Complete 5 challenges to earn the Phase-Master's Sigil, then explore in free-play mode as long as you like.

📈 The Heating Curve Chamber
"The curve never lies. It tells you exactly when the bonds break, when the particles fly, and when the temperature simply stops to wait."
Tier —
Question —/—
Score —
Welcome to the Heating Curve Chamber. The curve below tells the whole story of what happens when ice is slowly heated all the way to steam. Your task is to read it.

You will face three tiers of trials, each containing 5 questions. The graph is always on screen — you just click the part of it that answers the question.

Tier 1 — Read the Phases. Identify where water is solid, liquid, or gas, and where it's changing.
Tier 2 — Find the Key Points. Locate the melting point, boiling point, and the exact moments phase changes begin and end.
Tier 3 — Reason About Energy. The hardest tier. Where is energy breaking bonds? Where do particles move fastest? Where does heat enter without raising temperature?

Pass each tier with 4 of 5. A perfect 5 of 5 in every tier earns the Curve-Caller's Crest.
Pass each tier to unlock the next. Earn 5 of 5 on all three to claim the Curve-Caller's Crest.

🏁 ENERGY KART RACE
YOUR GOAL: Race down the track in 90 seconds. A prompt — like "Light Energy" — appears at the top. Three objects roll toward you. Drive over the one that matches!
STEER
A / ← slide left
D / → slide right
CONTROL
P pause
Esc quit race
⚠ CRASH PENALTY Hitting the wrong object freezes you for 5 seconds. You can crash — just don't run out the clock.

🌊 THE TUNING PIER — MATCH THE WAVE
"Listen to the sea. Tune your rope until the two waves are one."
🪙 0
🌊 0
★ Best 0/5

THE TUNING PIER

Two sliders control the rope wave at your feet: FREQUENCY (how often crests pass) and AMPLITUDE (how tall each crest stands). The dashed wave is the target tide you must match. As you tune, watch the live WAVELENGTH shrink when frequency rises — they are inversely linked. Watch the ENERGY meter rise with either taller waves OR more frequent ones — both carry more energy. Round 5 takes two waves at once.
Round 1 of 5
TIDE-SCOPE f = 1.00 Hz A = 1.00 m λ = 1.00 m
FREQUENCY 1.00 Hz
AMPLITUDE 1.00 m
ENERGY 0%

🔔 THE SOUNDING COVE — THE ECHO STONES
"Sound needs something to travel through. Bend it, bounce it — and learn which path it likes best."
🪙 0
🔔 0
★ Best 0/6

THE ECHO STONES

Two trials, six challenges. First, READ THE COVE: each scene is a wave doing one of four things — REFLECTION, REFRACTION, DIFFRACTION, or ABSORPTION. A badge tells you whether it's a SOUND or a LIGHT example; name the behaviour. Then, THE MEDIUM RACE: order air, water, and a solid pylon by how fast sound arrives. Remember — sound travels FASTER in denser media (the opposite of light).
Challenge 1 of 6
🔊 SOUND
Which wave behaviour does this describe?

🎐 THE CONCH CARILLON — VOICE THE SEA
"Loudness is how TALL the wave stands. Pitch is how OFTEN its crests come. Voice me the sea's moods."
🪙 0
🎐 0
★ Best 0/6

THE CONCH CARILLON

Six calls. For each, Cymbeline asks for a wave by its volume and pitch — like "a loud, low call." Use the two sliders to voice it: AMPLITUDE sets loudness (taller wave = louder), and FREQUENCY sets pitch (more crests = higher). You'll see and hear the wave change as you drag. Land both in the right range to ring the shells true.
Call 1 of 6

AMPLITUDE → loudness Loudness: MEDIUM
softmediumloud
FREQUENCY → pitch Pitch: MEDIUM
lowmediumhigh

🔮 THE SPECTRUM TIDE POOLS
"Seven pools, seven waves. Lay them out longest to shortest, then read what each one is good for."
🪙 0
🔮 0
★ Best 0/7

THE SPECTRUM TIDE POOLS

Two trials. First, drag the seven electromagnetic waves into order along the spectrum — from longest wavelength (lowest energy) to shortest wavelength (highest energy). Then each pool becomes a band of the spectrum: a card will rise describing a real use or property, and you drag it into the pool it belongs to. Sort all seven to clear the pools.
Trial 1 of 2 — Order the Spectrum
Drag each wave into a slot, longest wavelength on the left, shortest on the right.
◀ LONGER WAVELENGTH · LOWER ENERGY SHORTER WAVELENGTH · HIGHER ENERGY ▶
Wave bank
 
Trial 2 of 2 — Match the Use · 1 of 7
Drag the card into the matching tide pool.
 

TRIALS COMPLETE

🔦 THE LIGHTHOUSE LENS LAB
"Read the optic, predict the beam, and the lamp finds its way to sea."
🪙 0
🔦 0
★ Best 0/6

PREDICT THE BEAM

A beam of light strikes one optic — a lens or a mirror — from an angle. Your job: click the zone where the beam goes next.

Two questions in every shot. Which side? Light passes through a lens (it refracts — exits the far side) but bounces off a mirror (it reflects — comes back the near side). Which height? A converging optic pulls the beam toward the middle; a diverging optic spreads it outward, away from the center.
Converge → middle: convex lens, concave mirror
Diverge → outward: concave lens, convex mirror
Lens → far side (light refracts through)
Mirror → near side (light reflects back)
Beam 1 of 6
 
Converge → middle: convex lens · concave mirror
Diverge → outward: concave lens · convex mirror
Lens → far side (refracts through)
Mirror → near side (reflects back)

Fly by reaction. Hold an arrow key to fire that thruster — the exhaust shoves you the OPPOSITE way. Fire DOWN to rise, LEFT to go right. Pass all 5 gates in order.

▼exhaust down

🧲 Field Mapper Start at the green dot by N. Follow the red compass needle and tap to drop markers. Reach the gold target by S to map the line.
LINES 0/4
🪙 0
A compass needle always points along the magnet's field. Click the green START dot by the North pole, then keep tapping along the red needle — each tap drops a marker. Walk the markers all the way to the gold TARGET by the South pole to map one field line. Map 4 lines to finish.
🔌 Circuit Workshop — Sort the Circuit Drag each material to the right circuit: insulators on the left, conductors on the right.
sorted 0/16
✓ 0
🪙 0
Sort all 16 materials into the two circuits. Conductors (metals — loose electrons) let charge flow, so they go in the Electricity circuit on the right and its bulb lights. Insulators (rubber, glass, wood, plastic, air — locked electrons) block the flow, so they go in the No Electricity circuit on the left. Drag each item — or tap it, then tap a circuit. Wrong drops stick, so choose carefully.
💡 No Electricity
insulators — no charge flows
💡
⚡ Electricity
conductors — charge flows
💡
⚙️ Turbine Hall Drag the magnet to spin it. Faster spin = more voltage. Hold the gauge high enough to power each device.
⚡ 0.0 V
🪙 0
A generator's voltage depends on three things multiplied together: how fast the magnet spins, how many turns are in the coil, and how strong the magnet is. Drag the magnet to spin it (it coasts like a flywheel). Cranking alone will light the Bulb — but to run the Motor and power the whole Town you'll need to add coils and a stronger magnet too.
Coils 2
Magnet 2
🪐 Gravity Wing — Field Tuner More mass = stronger pull. More distance = weaker pull (a lot weaker). Match each target and hold it.
🪐 pull 0.0
🪙 0
Two things — and only two — decide how hard gravity pulls: how much mass there is, and how far apart the bodies are. Turn Mass up for a stronger pull. Turn Distance up for a weaker pull — and distance matters fast: double the distance and the pull drops to a quarter. Set both dials so the pull lands in the green band for each target, and hold steady.
Mass 3
Distance 5
⚒️ THE AETHER FABRICATOR
"Bring raw materials. Leave with something greater."
Build crafted objects from the materials you mine across the realms. The right materials are chosen for what they do — that choice is the science. Finished objects go in your pack; carry them to whoever asked for them.
